chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permitted to Be Slaughtered in the Sanctuary and Taken Outside

parts.text: 2384 - وَ رَوَى عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ

text: : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِقَتْلِ اَلنَّمْلِ وَ اَلْبَقِّ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ » وَ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِقَتْلِ اَلْقَمْلَةِ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ وَ غَيْرِهِ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2384 - Muawiya ibn Ammar narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said: "There is no harm in killing ants and bedbugs in the sacred sanctuary (Al-Haram)."
Imam (as) also said: "There is no harm in killing lice both within the sanctuary and outside of it."
